description: EtcdBackup is the Schema for the etcdbackups API

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: EtcdBackupSpec defines the desired state of EtcdBackup

clusterRef.description: ClusterRef references the EtcdCluster to back up.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

destination.description: Destination defines where the backup will be stored.

pvc.description: PVC defines a PersistentVolumeClaim as the backup destination.

claimName.description: ClaimName is the name of the PersistentVolumeClaim to use.

subPath.description:
SubPath is an optional sub-directory within the PVC volume.
The operator appends the backup filename automatically.

s3.description: S3 defines S3-compatible storage as the backup destination.

bucket.description: Bucket is the name of the S3 bucket.

credentialsSecretRef.description: CredentialsSecretRef references a Secret containing AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY keys.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

endpoint.description: Endpoint is the S3-compatible endpoint URL (e.g., "https://s3.amazonaws.com").

forcePathStyle.description:
ForcePathStyle forces path-style S3 URLs (e.g., endpoint/bucket/key)
instead of virtual-hosted-style (e.g., bucket.endpoint/key).
Most S3-compatible providers (MinIO, Ceph) require path style.

key.description:
Key is the key prefix (directory path) within the bucket.
The operator appends the backup filename automatically.

region.description: Region is the AWS region for the S3 bucket.

status.description: EtcdBackupStatus defines the observed state of EtcdBackup

items.description: Condition contains details for one aspect of the current state of this API Resource.

lastTransitionTime.description:
lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
This should be when the underlying condition changed.  If that is not known, then using the time when the API field changed is acceptable.

message.description:
message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.
This may be an empty string.

observedGeneration.description:
observedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon.
For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions[x].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date
with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason.description:
reason contains a programmatic identifier indicating the reason for the condition's last transition.
Producers of specific condition types may define expected values and meanings for this field,
and whether the values are considered a guaranteed API.
The value should be a CamelCase string.
This field may not be empty.

status.description: status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

type.description: type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.

snapshot.description:
Snapshot describes the artefact the backup-agent wrote.
Populated by the controller after the backup Job reaches
phase=Succeeded, parsed from the agent's terminal
"snapshot uploaded: ..." / "snapshot written: ..." pod-log
marker. Empty while the backup is in progress, on failure
before the write step, or when the Job pod's log has been GC'd
before the controller observed it.

checksum.description:
Checksum is "<algo>:<hex>" of the snapshot bytes, computed by
the agent while streaming. Currently always "sha256:<hex>"
(64 hex chars) when the controller populates BackupSnapshot at
all; consumers MUST tolerate other algorithms via the prefix.
The controller's marker regex requires sha256=<64 hex> to be
present, so this field is non-empty whenever the enclosing
BackupSnapshot is set.

The pattern admits hyphenated algorithm names (e.g.
"sha3-256:...", "blake2b-256:...", "blake3-256:...") so a
future agent build can adopt a stronger hash without an API
break, and bounds the hex run to 32–128 chars (128-bit to
512-bit digests) so a truncated emit cannot satisfy the
pattern with a meaningless short hash.

sizeBytes.description:
SizeBytes is the snapshot size in bytes, as observed by the
backup-agent at write time. Populated whenever the enclosing
BackupSnapshot is set: a successful marker parse always
produces a value here. The only way SizeBytes==0 lands with
BackupSnapshot set is the strconv.ParseInt overflow fallback
in the marker scanner — i.e. an agent-emitted size larger
than int64, which is unreachable in practice. In that one
case the scanner deliberately STILL lands a populated
BackupSnapshot (with SizeBytes=0, URI and Checksum
preserved) rather than discarding it: the URI + checksum are
the load-bearing fields for a future restore, and a
reviewer reading status sees the snapshot exists. When the
agent log was GC'd or never contained a marker, the entire
BackupSnapshot stays nil rather than being set with a zero
SizeBytes.

uri.description:
URI is an OBSERVATIONAL identifier of the snapshot the agent
wrote — intended for humans, dashboards, and out-of-band
tooling. For S3 destinations: "s3://<bucket>/<key>" where
<key> is the FULL object name (with any BACKUP_INCLUDE_REVISION
/ BACKUP_TIMESTAMP suffix the agent injected at write time).
For PVC destinations: "file:///<abs-path>" — note the triple
slash, which is the literal output of joining the "file://"
scheme with an absolute in-container path. The path is
in-container (rooted at the operator-injected /backup/data
mount of spec.destination.pvc.claimName) and is not directly
readable from outside the pod.

This field is NOT a stable contract for programmatic restore
callers. The S3 form omits endpoint / region / path-style flags
that are required to address non-AWS endpoints (MinIO, Ceph
RGW, Wasabi, ...) and the PVC form omits the PVC claimName and
namespace. A future RestoreSpec consumer must read those values
from the originating EtcdBackup.spec.destination — not from
this string. The URI format may evolve in subsequent versions
to expose richer addressing; consumers should treat unfamiliar
schemes as opaque rather than parsing.

KEEP IN SYNC with the SnapshotURIPrefixPattern constant in this
package — the controller compiles its defense-in-depth regex
from that constant; if this marker drifts, the controller will
admit URIs that the apiserver refuses to write and reconciles
will spin on Status().Update validation errors.
